description_long: This object is a discarded coffee cup resembling a typical takeaway cup, with a conical body and a fitted lid. The surface is worn and dirty, showing signs of weathering and urban grime, with visible scratches and stains. On one side, there is a green and white circular logo parodying the Starbucks brand, reading 'BUCKSBUCKS COFFEE' with dollar signs in the center. The lid is slightly recessed with a molded drinking slot, and the cup appears to be made of a lightweight, rigid material, likely coated paper or thin plastic. The overall color is a faded, dirty white with patches of gray and brown from accumulated dirt. The cup is empty and crumpled, suggesting it has been discarded and exposed to the elements.
description: A dirty, discarded takeaway coffee cup with a parody green logo and a fitted lid, showing signs of wear and grime.
description_view_0: Front view showing the 'BUCKSBUCKS COFFEE' logo and the drinking slot on the lid.
description_view_1: Rear view with no visible logo, highlighting the worn, dirty surface and the full lid.
description_view_2: Side view showing the cup's conical shape, lid details, and extensive surface grime.
description_view_3: Angled view with part of the logo visible, emphasizing the cup's weathered texture and lid.
attribute_brand_logo: A green and white circular logo parodying Starbucks, with the text 'BUCKSBUCKS COFFEE' and two dollar signs in the center.
attribute_rust_or_damage_marks: Surface is dirty and scratched with visible grime and wear, but no major dents or rust.
